div1.entryFree: Merriment, diversion, amusement; mirth: rather proved the sliding of your brother a m. than a vice, Meas. 2, 4, 2, 4 they do it but in mocking m. LLL V, 2, 139. knowing aforehand of our m. LLL V, 2, 139 thou interruptest our m. LLL V, 2, 139 met your loves like a m. LLL V, 2, 139 stir up the Athenian youth to --s, Mids. I, 1, 12. for your m. III, 2, 146. friends that purpose m. Merch. II, 2, 212. frame your mind to mirth and m. Shr. Ind. 2, 137. our first m. hath made thee jealous, IV, 5, 76. strain their cheeks to idle m. John III, 3, 46. turn all to a m. H4B II, 4, 324. nature's tears are reason's m. Rom. IV, 5, 83. your flashes of m. Hml. V, 1, 210.
